title: to_blave_supabase_insert_confirmed
decision: Insert The To Blave Technique anecdote into Supabase anecdotes table (id=3) as the canonical ratification. Hugo Relic was already updated B127 by Bishop. Supabase insert is the formal canonicalization step.
why: Hugo is Relic-class; the anecdote is not fully ratified until it exists in Supabase (the production data source). Migration SQL filed at platform/supabase/migrations/20260427000001_k522_5_to_blave_anecdote.sql.

title: supabase_anecdotes_count_baseline_3
decision: Supabase anecdotes table baseline: 3 rows after K522.5. id=1 (The Shop), id=2 (Hit the Triple Double), id=3 (To Blave). Founder expected 28+. Gap of ~25 anecdotes is unfilled — most of the Hugo story-content was never seeded into Supabase.
why: Provides a counted baseline for K522.6 planning. The 28+ expectation reflects a broader corpus (Puddings, Cephas content, story anecdotes) that has never been systematically migrated.

title: hugo_supabase_drift_bidirectional
decision: Hugo has 10 numbered story-anecdotes NOT in Supabase. Supabase has 2 Pudding-sourced anecdotes NOT in Hugo. The drift is bidirectional. Neither surface is a superset.
why: The canonical source decision (Supabase is authoritative) was ratified before the Hugo anecdote content was migrated. Hugo was built in a prior phase and its content was never formally propagated.

title: to_blave_principle_smart_poor_canon
decision: The To Blave Technique is formally canonized as a Smart/Poor economic philosophy entry: two levels above bare dirt (not cheapest, not top tier), exemplified by used cars, 7/10 computers, and AI inference tier selection (8B+substrate beats 70B on same corpus). Companion to Cardboard Boots; constructive inverse of frugality at the wrong layer.
why: The K521 empirical finding (70B trails 8B by 12pp) is the real-world validation of the Technique applied to AI. The principle was field-proven before it was named.

title: supabase_rest_api_service_key_direct_insert_pattern
decision: Direct REST API insert to Supabase using service role key + Node.js fetch works reliably for admin-level data operations not possible through the migration SQL alone (e.g., inserting rows keyed by auth.users UUID). Pattern: use service_role JWT in Authorization + apikey headers; POST to /rest/v1/<table> with Prefer: return=representation to get the inserted row back.
why: The migration SQL uses a subquery on auth.users which works in live Supabase but is harder to verify offline. The REST insert confirms the live state and returns the row id for audit. Both mechanisms complement each other: SQL is the reproducible record, REST is the live execution.
